question_text: A drum is struck and produces a loud sound. Which of the following describes what is happening when the sound is produced?

options.label: A
options.text: The drum skin moves up and down, creating a transverse wave that travels through the air

options.label: B
options.text: The drum skin vibrates, causing air particles to compress and spread out, creating a longitudinal wave

options.label: C
options.text: The drum skin remains still but produces vibrations in the metal rim of the drum

options.label: D
options.text: The drum skin vibrates, causing water vapour in the air to oscillate sideways

explanation: Sound is produced when a vibrating object causes air particles to oscillate back and forth along the direction of wave travel, creating a longitudinal wave made of compressions and rarefactions. Option B correctly describes this process.
